element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic for ball control and aggression without penalty
Defensive strategy distinct from standard basketball
Environmental obstacles that cause ball handler to fall and lose possession
Referee acts as moveable obstacle on court
High-risk jump attack that tackles ball carrier; failure causes player to roll on floor
Next basket wins; multiple 1-minute periods if no score

typical_tension_triggers: Score deficit with time running down
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Constant aggressive contact encouraged; tension escalates through quarter progression; sudden spikes during overtime

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-like quarter 1 where player learns basic mechanics and aggression is introduced
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Quarters 2-3 escalate opponent AI aggression and hazard frequency; player must balance offense and defense
difficulty_curve.late_game: Quarter 4 intensifies with tight scoring and increased opponent pressure; tie scenarios lead to sudden death overtime with extreme tension
notable_spikes: Sudden death overtime activation (next basket wins)
Computer opponent aggression ramp in final quarter
Hazard density increases in arcade version

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as 4 quarters of 4 minutes each (regulation). Standard basketball rhythm with quarter breaks
If tied after regulation, 1-minute overtime periods repeat until next basket scored. Escalates tension exponentially
Full court becomes obstacle course with soda cans and candy wrappers that trip ball handlers. Referee also acts as collision hazard
Team selection randomized; player is home team (Player 1), opponent is away team. Creates varied matchups without progression
